title: Architecture as Inductive Bias & Interpretability
summary: The architecture defines the functional form of $q_\theta$, determining which parameters exist and how they interact. This constrains the solution space and provides the framework through which humans understand what the model has learned.
key_insights: Supervised learning minimizes $D_{KL}(p \| q_\theta)$ — the objective doesn't care about architecture
Architecture defines which function family $q_\theta$ belongs to: $Q_{\text{transformer}}, Q_{\text{CNN}}, Q_{\text{linear}}$
Architecture makes the learned solution interpretable: embeddings → geometry, attention → patterns, MLP → detectors
Architecture = inductive bias = constraints that shape what kind of $q_\theta$ can be learned
Without architecture, $q_\theta$ = giant lookup table — uninterpretable. With architecture = structured latent space

mathematical_core.architecture_role: Defines the set $Q$ such that $q_\theta \in Q_{\text{architecture}}$

derivations.title: KL divergence as the unifying training objective

steps.name: Training equivalence
steps.math: Since $H(p)$ is constant w.r.t. $\theta$: $\arg\min_\theta D_{KL}(p \| q_\theta) = \arg\min_\theta \text{CE}(p, q_\theta)$

steps.name: Architecture independence
steps.math: This objective is valid for ANY $q_\theta$. The architecture only constrains WHICH $q_\theta$ functions are reachable, not the objective.

derivations.consequence: Cross-entropy loss IS KL minimization. Architecture determines the search space, loss determines the direction.

derivations.title: Inductive bias as function space restriction
derivations.math: Universal approximation: a sufficiently large MLP can approximate any function. But transformers approximate SOME functions much more efficiently because their structure matches the data. The 'bias' is toward functions that decompose into attention (relational) + FFN (transformational) + residual (refinement) patterns.
derivations.consequence: Architecture choice = betting that your data has the structure your architecture is biased toward.

application_relevance: When adapting an architecture, you are choosing which aspects of the solution will be interpretable. This is the theoretical foundation for architectural innovation.
